lesson.title: CAPS.PhysicalSciences.Gr11.PHY.1: Resolution of vectors into components. Addition by compon...
lesson.explanation: Kinematics describes motion without considering forces. Key concepts: displacement (change in position, can be negative), velocity (rate of change of displacement), acceleration (rate of change of velocity). Equations of motion: v = u + at, s = ut + ½at², v² = u² + 2as. Motion graphs: x-t graphs show position over time (gradient = velocity), v-t graphs show velocity (gradient = acceleration, area = displacement).

practice_problems.question: Resolve a 50 N force acting at 30 degrees above the horizontal into horizontal and vertical components.
hints: Horizontal component: Fx = F cos theta.
Vertical component: Fy = F sin theta.

practice_problems.question: Two forces act on an object: 40 N east and 30 N north. Calculate the magnitude and direction of the resultant.
practice_problems.answer: R = sqrt(40^2 + 30^2) = sqrt(2500) = 50 N. Angle = tan^-1(30/40) = 36.9 degrees north of east.
hints: Use Pythagoras for perpendicular vectors: R = sqrt(Rx^2 + Ry^2).
practice_problems.explanation: R = sqrt(1600 + 900) = sqrt(2500) = 50 N. theta = tan^-1(30/40) = tan^-1(0.75) = 36.9 degrees north of east.

practice_problems.question: A crate rests on a 25-degree inclined plane. Its weight is 200 N. Calculate the component of the weight parallel to the slope and perpendicular to the slope.
practice_problems.answer: W_parallel = mg sin(25) = 200 sin(25) = 84.5 N. W_perpendicular = mg cos(25) = 200 cos(25) = 181.3 N.
hints: On an incline: parallel component = mg sin theta, perpendicular = mg cos theta.
The angle of the incline equals the angle between the weight vector and the perpendicular.
practice_problems.explanation: W_par = 200 x sin(25) = 200 x 0.4226 = 84.5 N (pulls crate down the slope). W_perp = 200 x cos(25) = 200 x 0.9063 = 181.3 N (pushes into the slope).

practice_problems.question: Three forces act on an object: 60 N east, 40 N at 120 degrees from east, and 30 N south. Find the resultant using component addition.
hints: Resolve each force into x and y components.
Sum all x-components and all y-components separately.

practice_problems.question: An airplane flies at 250 km/h on a bearing of 060 degrees. A crosswind blows from the west at 50 km/h. Find the resultant velocity (magnitude and direction).
practice_problems.answer: Plane: Vx = 250sin(60) = 216.5, Vy = 250cos(60) = 125. Wind: Vx = 50, Vy = 0 (from west = eastward). Total: Vx = 266.5, Vy = 125. V = sqrt(266.5^2 + 125^2) = 294.4 km/h. Bearing = 90 - tan^-1(125/266.5) = 90 - 25.1 = 064.9 degrees (approximately).
hints: Resolve the plane's velocity using: east = V sin(bearing), north = V cos(bearing).
practice_problems.explanation: Complex vector addition with bearings. The wind pushes the plane further east, slightly changing direction and increasing ground speed.

hints: What concept from Vectors in Two Dimensions applies here?
Apply the method for Vectors in Two Dimensions step by step.
Start by writing down what you know about Vectors in Two Dimensions.

misconceptions.misconception: A common error in Vectors in Two Dimensions.
misconceptions.why_students_think_this: This mistake is common because students often skip a key step.

assessment.title: Assessment: Resolution of vectors into components. Addition by components. Newton's laws in 2D with resolved forces. Inclined planes. Friction (static and kinetic)
